stem:
P(t) = 260(1.04)^((6/4)t)

The function P models the population, in thousands, of a certain city t years after 2003. According to the model, the population is predicted to increase by 4% every n months. What is the value of n ?
explanation: The base 1.04 means the population grows 4% each time the exponent (6/4)t increases by 1. Setting (6/4)t = 1 gives t = 4/6 = 2/3 of a year. Since a year is 12 months, 2/3 of a year is 8 months, so n = 8.
